steps.title: Let's build a Correct Order slide!
steps.description: A Correct Order slide challenges participants to arrange shuffled statements in the right sequence — perfect for timelines, processes, and ranked lists. We'll build one together in under a minute. Click Next to begin.

steps.title: Create a new presentation
steps.description: Click 'New Presentation' to open a fresh deck.

steps.title: Pick the Correct Order slide type
steps.description: Select 'Correct Order' from the slide type picker in the Quiz section.

steps.title: Write your question
steps.description: Type the challenge for your audience. A question, a task, or simple instructions — whatever makes the sequence clear to participants.

action.value: Put these events in chronological order

steps.title: Add your statements
steps.description: Enter each statement in the correct order — AhaSlides shuffles them automatically for participants. Pro tip: keep it to 5 items maximum for the smoothest drag-and-drop experience on mobile.

steps.title: Pro tip: make each item distinct
steps.description: Avoid statements that could plausibly swap positions — each item should have only one correct placement in the sequence. Clear, unique items make for a fairer and more satisfying quiz.

steps.title: Configure time and points
steps.description: Scroll down to set the time limit (default: 45 seconds), reward points, and whether faster answers earn more points. Pro tip: a logical sequence — timeline, process steps, or ranked list — gives participants a fair shot.

steps.title: Set participant language to English
steps.description: Open Settings to confirm the audience display language is set to English — so participants see all text in English on their devices.

steps.title: Preview — see what your participants will see
steps.description: Click Preview to open a side-by-side view of the presenter and participant screens.

mainInteractions.label: open preview via JS click (bypasses overlay backdrop)

steps.title: Participants drag statements into order
steps.description: When your audience joins, they see the shuffled statements and drag them into the sequence they think is correct. They tap Submit when done — all within the time limit you set.

steps.title: Results appear live on your screen
steps.description: The presenter view shows a real-time leaderboard and the correct order revealed. Hit Present when you're ready to go live — participants will race to arrange the statements correctly on their phones.
